draft.title: a side project tracker
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a side project tracker

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer immediate response over smooth animation when tracking side projects
facade.label: Instant snap
facade.acceptImplies: Clicks and state changes feel snappy and direct, no easing or delay
facade.rejectImplies: Transitions and animations should feel fluid and considered, not abrupt

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer approachable, friendly interfaces over sharp geometric layouts for a personal project tracker
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Explore friendly, tactile aesthetic; curved cards, gentle borders, organic button shapes
facade.rejectImplies: Test sharper, more structured aesthetic; angular cards, crisp lines, geometric precision

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer immediate response over smooth animation when tracking side projects
evidence.implication: Clicks and state changes feel snappy and direct, no easing or delay

facade.hypothesis: A friendly, encouraging tone with celebratory language makes side project tracking feel rewarding rather than like a chore
facade.label: Cheerful check-ins
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward warm, motivational language; celebrates small wins; wants personality in UI copy
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers neutral, business-like tone; finds cheerfulness distracting; wants minimal, straightforward interface language

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer visual momentum (completion percentage) over narrative status updates when tracking side projects
facade.label: Progress bar
facade.acceptImplies: User values quantified, at-a-glance project health over descriptive text; likely wants dashboard density and metric-driven design
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers qualitative status (e.g., 'On track', 'Blocked', 'Brainstorming') or written context over numerical progress indicators; likely wants narrative clarity and human language

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a persistent left sidebar for project filters paired with card-based task display in the main area, reducing cognitive load from tab switching
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: User values persistent context and quick filtering; likely wants to see multiple projects at once; may prefer visual scanning over deep nesting
facade.rejectImplies: User finds sidebars cramped or wants full-width focus; prefers tab-based navigation or list-only layouts; prioritizes simplicity over persistent navigation

facade.hypothesis: Users building side projects want clear visual separation between navigation and content—a calm, organized feeling rather than an open, minimal aesthetic
facade.axisTargeted: calm vs energetic + organic vs digital
facade.label: Dark sidebar, bright cards
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward defined structure, contrast, and visual breathing room. Likely prefers grounded, slightly formal interfaces over flat minimalism.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer softer transitions, unified color palettes, or less hierarchical layouts. Possibly drawn to seamless, borderless design or monochromatic approaches.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich visual feedback and status indicators for their side projects
Whether user wants quick-access dashboard view vs deep nested organization for managing multiple concurrent projects
Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs straightforward/no-nonsense communication about progress
Whether user wants a focused, distraction-free environment vs an energizing, visually stimulating workspace
Whether user prioritizes seeing all project metrics at once vs progressive disclosure of details on demand
Whether user prefers instant feedback and snappy interactions vs smooth, deliberate transitions between states

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich visual feedback and status indicators for their side projects
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-access dashboard view vs deep nested organization for managing multiple concurrent projects
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs straightforward/no-nonsense communication about progress
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a focused, distraction-free environment vs an energizing, visually stimulating workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes seeing all project metrics at once vs progressive disclosure of details on demand
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers instant feedback and snappy interactions vs smooth, deliberate transitions between states
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users want tactile, gesture-based control for managing project priorities rather than menu-driven interactions
facade.axisTargeted: Motion and Behavior — gesture-driven interaction vs. menu-based control
facade.label: Drag to reorder
facade.acceptImplies: Design favors fluid drag interactions, long-press gestures, and real-time visual feedback for task manipulation; prioritize smooth animations and haptic-like responsiveness
facade.rejectImplies: Design should emphasize menu options, keyboard shortcuts, or modal dialogs for task management; prioritize stability and predictability over tactile fluidity
